test_output_summary: 304 passed, 1 pre-existing failure (test_strip_ac_prefix_with_checkbox in test_coach_validator_fuzzy.py — pre-existed before this task's changes, confirmed via git stash verification) in 1.34s
implementation_notes: Added _strip_markdown_formatting() static method to CoachValidator between _extract_keywords() and _match_by_text(). The method uses re.sub to remove backticks, double quotes, single quotes, and Unicode smart quotes (“”) but deliberately does NOT strip underscores, which are significant in identifiers like log_level. Applied the new method in _match_by_text() at two points: (1) to the requirements_met list after _strip_criterion_prefix() stripping, and (2) to each acceptance_criteria entry after _strip_criterion_prefix() stripping. The re module was already imported at line 36. _strip_criterion_prefix() and _match_by_promises() were not modified.
concerns: One pre-existing test failure exists: test_strip_ac_prefix_with_checkbox in tests/unit/test_coach_validator_fuzzy.py. This test was already in the modified working-directory state before this task started (test_coach_validator_fuzzy.py appears in git status as modified). The test expects _strip_criterion_prefix('- [ ] AC-001: Settings class has log_level field') to return 'AC-001: Settings class has log_level field', but the current implementation strips both the checkbox AND the AC prefix, returning 'Settings class has log_level field'. This is unrelated to TASK-FIX-TM03 and may be part of TASK-FIX-TM04.
requirements_addressed: Add _strip_markdown_formatting() static method that strips backticks and quotes but NOT underscores

completion_promises.criterion_text: Add a new _strip_markdown_formatting() static method that strips backticks and quotes but NOT underscores
completion_promises.evidence: Added @staticmethod _strip_markdown_formatting(text: str) -> str at line 1885 in coach_validator.py. Uses re.sub(r'[`"\u2018\u2019\u201c\u201d]', '', text) to strip backtick, double quote, single quote, and smart quotes. Underscore is not in the character class and is therefore preserved.

Added 'stripped_met = [self._strip_markdown_formatting(r) for r in stripped_met]' immediately after the _strip_criterion_prefix() pass over requirements_met, so both stripping passes apply before the normalized_met set is built.

Added 'stripped_criterion = self._strip_markdown_formatting(stripped_criterion)' immediately after the _strip_criterion_prefix() call for each criterion_text in the loop, before the .lower().strip() normalization that builds the 'normalized' variable.
